titleStmt.title: The United States Deputy Representative on the North Atlantic Council ( (Spofford) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The United States Deputy Representative on the North Atlantic Council ( Spofford ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
Depto 155. Hoyer-MillarDerick Hoyer Millar, U.K. Deputy Representative on the North Atlantic Council. and RoseEdward M. Rose, of the Western Union Secretariat of the British Foreign Office. of Foreign Office state that Bevin has not yet reached decision on “where we go next” after Defense
Committee meeting pending full discussion with Shinwell who returned only yesterday. Foreign Office however thinking tentatively along following lines: 1.
Earliest conclusion of Schuman Plan most desirable but British fear any pressure from them to this end might not be well received. They would welcome US action. 2.
French must obviously be persuaded to negotiate, and only sound basis for negotiation would be American proposals, incorporating additional safeguards and taking account of both French and German views. 3.
Ways must be found of saving French Government’s face. Pleven Plan was obviously completely impracticable but could not be publicly rejected without freezing this and future French Governments in opposition. If any parts of it could be soundly adapted and incorporated firmly in NATO framework, so much the better. If not, perhaps parts could be accepted in principle as goals to be worked toward rather than as conditions precedent to prompt action. 4.
British had gained impression in Washington we considered early solution German participation issue necessary in connection with our legislative program and would welcome further information as to our thinking on timing and legislative schedule. 5.
They stated Bevin had made no visible impression or progress with Guy Mollet.Secretary General of the Socialist Party in France, appointed Minister of State for European Affairs in Pleven Cabinet, July 12, 1950.
Sent Department Depto 155, repeated info Paris 833, Frankfort 350.
